# Repository metadata (fill these into GitHub Settings > Repository details)

Use this file as a copy-paste source when updating the GitHub repository "About" panel and topics.

- Repository name: F3Go30
- Short description (one line): Google Apps Script automation for monthly Go30 tracker spreadsheets
- Website / Homepage: https://github.com/stuartdonaldson/F3Go30 (or project docs link)
- Topics (comma-separated): apps-script, google-sheets, automation, go30, fitness, beads, docs
- Default branch: main
- License: MIT (see LICENSE file)
- Visibility: Public (or Private as desired)
- README location: README.md (root)
- Documentation landing page: docs/CONTEXT.md
- Issue tracker: Enabled (use Beads locally for repo-scoped tracking; see `.beads/README.md`)
- Code of conduct: none (optional)
- Contributing guide: CONTRIBUTING.md (create if needed)

Suggested repository description (longer):
F3Go30 automates the monthly lifecycle of a Go30 fitness challenge tracker in Google Sheets: copies a template spreadsheet and its bound Google Form, initializes sheets and triggers, shortens and surfaces URLs, logs operations for audit and developer verification, and provides owner-only menu actions for easy month-to-month operation.

Suggested topics explanation to paste into GitHub Topics UI:
- apps-script — code is Google Apps Script (bound to Sheets)
- google-sheets — primary runtime environment is Google Sheets
- automation — automates repetitive spreadsheet setup tasks
- go30 — domain: 30-day Go30 fitness challenge tracker
- fitness — domain tag for discoverability
- beads — recommended local issue tracker included in `.beads/`
- docs — repo contains documentation templates and generated doc artifacts

Optional: add homepage field linking to `docs/CONTEXT.md` or a project wiki if you host elsewhere.
